examples: I saw that she was, both literally and figuratively, up against a wall.
It is figuratively and literally otherworldly in its aims.
Dependence on long-distance trade also meant that the great empires of the Middle East were built both literally and figuratively on shifting sand.
They can figuratively try to ride the movement as if it were a bucking bronco.
Why is literature still figuratively the nervous dude in the tweed jacket smelling of moth balls?
